options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: goal
options.description:
Determines what must be done to complete the game.
[Creator] Ascend Vermillion Tower and fight the Creator.
[Monkey] Ascend the Grand Krys'kajo and defeat the Son of the East.
[Observatory] Complete all five A Promise is a Promise quests and uncover the secret of the Observatory.
[Di'orbis] Descend Ku'lero temple and defeat the Gods of Shape. Requires DLC to be enabled.

options.name: enable_dlc
options.description: If enabled, DLC areas will be placed in logic. Requires that you have the DLC installed.

options.name: circuit_overrides
options.description: Number of circuit overrides to shuffle into the pool.

options.name: vt_shade_lock
options.description:
If set to a non-None value, creates an in-game barrier at the entrance of Vermillion Tower to prevent extremely
quick playthroughs.
[Bosses] Vermillion Tower only opens when the bosses at the end of the first four dungeons have been beaten.
[Shades] Vermillion Tower only opens when the four shades acquired at the end of the first four dungeons have been
         acquired.
[Bosses and Shades] Vermillion Tower opens when both of the other conditions have been satisfied.

options.name: vw_meteor_passage
options.description: If enabled, places a gate between Sapphire Ridge and Vermillion Wasteland unlockable with the meteor shade,

options.name: vt_skip
options.description:
If enabled, Vermillion Tower will not need to be completed; instead, the player will skip through it to the final
boss.

options.name: closed_gaia
options.description:
If set to a non-None value, adds barriers to each side of Gaia's Garden to decrease the amount of immediate checks
when unlocking the area, openable with East and West Gaia Pass obtained from Apollo duel encounter.
[Minimal] Adds barriers to Left and Right side of Gaia (includes dungeons).
[Full] Besides the Left and Right barriers, also adds gates in Grove and Infested areas which require the dungeon shades.

options.name: rhombus_hub_unlock
options.description:
If enabled, allows traveling to areas out of order from the hub in Rhombus Square. Allows skipping areas such as
Autumn's Fall if you are missing the Red Flame Shade, but have the Green Seed Shade / Star Shade.

options.name: progressive_area_unlocks
options.description:
If enabled, the items that unlock overworld areas and dungeons (including shades and some passes) will be made
progressive.
[None] no areas are unlocked progressively.
[Dungeons] dungeons are unlocked progressively; overworld areas are not.
[Overworld] overworld areas are unlocked progressively; dungeons are not.
[Split] both dungeons and overworld areas are unlocked progressively in separate progressive chains.
[Combined] both dungeons and overworld areas are unlocked progressively from the same progressive chain.

options.name: progressive_equipment
options.description:
If enabled, equipment will be progressive, sorted into different categories according to playstyle depending on
which of the other location-adding options are enabled.

options.name: keyrings
options.description: If enabled, all keys for each dungeon will be replaced with a singular item that unlocks every door in that dungeon.

options.name: allow_booster_grinding
options.description:
If enabled, some locations may require grinding enemies with boosters on for gem drops.
If disabled, alternative methods for obtaining required items will be made available.

options.name: chest_reveal
options.description:
If enabled, shows the category of all chests without having to reach the room chests are in first.
If disabled, the player must reach the room with chests to know what category the chests are.

options.name: quest_rando
options.description: If enabled, all quest rewards will be added to the location list.

options.name: botanics_completion_amount
options.description: The number of plants required to turn in the quest "Crocus Pocus".

options.name: hidden_quest_reward_mode
options.description:
Some quests hide their rewards until they are completed.
[Vanilla] Behavior is unchanged from the base game.
[Show All] Show all rewards regardless of whether they're hidden in the base game.
[Hide All] Hide all rewards regardless of whether they're hidden in the base game.

options.name: hidden_quest_obfuscation_level
options.description:
For quests with hidden rewards, this option controls the level to which rewards are obscured.
[Hide Item] Only hides the item name. The icon and receiving player are still accurate.
[Hide Text] Obscures item name and receiving player. The icon will still be accurate.
[Hide All] The item name and receiving player will all be hidden and the icon will be replaced with a generic
           Archipelago logo.

options.name: quest_dialog_hints
options.description:
If enabled, upon viewing the quest dialog for a quest with rewards that are not hidden, hints are sent to the
Archipelago server for all non-filler quest rewards.

options.name: shop_rando
options.description: If enabled, all shops will be added to the location list.

options.name: shop_dialog_hints
options.description:
If enabled, upon opening the dialog for a shop, corresponding hints are sent to the Archipelago server for all
non-filler shop items.

options.name: shop_send_mode
options.description:
Controls what exactly counts as a check when shop randomization is enabled.
[Per Item Type] A check is added for each type of item (for example, Sandwich). Therefore, purchasing a sandwich
from any shop in the game clears the same check.
[Per Slot] A check is added for each item slot in each shop. Therefore, purchasing a Sandwich in Rookie Harbor
clears a separate check than purchasing a Sandwich in Bergen Village.

options.name: shop_receive_mode
options.description:
Controls how shops are unlocked when shop randomization is enabled.
[None] All shop slots are able to be purchased as soon as the player can access the shop.
[Per Item Type] A check is added for each type of item (for example, Sandwich) which unlocks the ability to purchase
that item from any shop.
[Per Shop] A check is added for each shop (for example, Rookie Harbor Items) which unlocks the ability to purchase
items from that shop.
[Per Slot] A check is added for each item slot in each shop which unlocks the ability to purchase that item from
that shop (this may lead to tedious playthroughs).

options.name: botanity
options.description: If enabled, acquiring all the information about each plant by collecting drops is a location.

options.name: start_with_green_leaf_shade
options.description:
If enabled, the player will start with the green leaf shade, unlocking Autumn's Fall. This makes the early game far
more open.

options.name: start_with_chest_detector
options.description: If enabled, the player will start with the chest detector item, which will notify them of the chests in the room.

options.name: start_with_discs
options.description:
If set to a value other than "none", the player will start with the corresponding disc items.
Disc of Insight unlocks the records menu.
Disc of Flora unlocks the botany menu, allowing the player to start collecting plant samples from the beginning.

options.name: start_with_pet
options.description: If enabled, the player will start with a random pet. This is just for fun.

options.name: shade_shuffle
options.description: Where shades will appear.

options.name: element_shuffle
options.description: Where elements will appear.

options.name: small_key_shuffle
options.description: Where small keys will appear.

options.name: master_key_shuffle
options.description: Where master keys will appear.

options.name: chest_key_shuffle
options.description:
Where the Thief's Key, White Key, and Radiant Key (the keys that open bronze, silver, and gold chests, respectively)
may appear.

options.name: chest_lock_randomization
options.description: If enabled, the lock on all chests (Bronze, Silver, Gold, or None) will be randomized.

options.name: no_chest_lock_weight
options.description: Controls the likelihood of giving a chest no lock (if chest lock randomization is enabled).

options.name: bronze_chest_lock_weight
options.description: Controls the likelihood of giving a chest bronze lock, requiring Thief's Key to open (if chest lock randomization is enabled).

options.name: silver_chest_lock_weight
options.description: Controls the likelihood of giving a chest silver lock, requiring White Key to open (if chest lock randomization is enabled).

options.name: gold_chest_lock_weight
options.description: Controls the likelihood of giving a chest gild lock, requiring Radiant Key to open (if chest lock randomization is enabled).

options.name: exclude_always_quests
options.description:
Certain quests are always in the location pool because they hold progression items when playing vanilla CrossCode.
If this option is selected (and quest rando is disabled), this option will ensure that none of those locations are
populated with progression or useful items. It will also prohibit items from being placed on NPC interactions that
give progression items but require working through part of a questline to get to.

options.name: common_pool_weight
options.description: Controls the likelihood of choosing a common filler item when filling the world.

options.name: rare_pool_weight
options.description: Controls the likelihood of choosing a rare filler item when filling the world.

options.name: epic_pool_weight
options.description: Controls the likelihood of choosing a epic filler item when filling the world.

options.name: legendary_pool_weight
options.description: Controls the likelihood of choosing a legendary filler item when filling the world.

options.name: consumable_weight
options.description: Controls the likelihood of choosing a consumable item (as opposed to a drop).

options.name: drop_weight
options.description: Controls the likelihood of choosing a drop item (as opposed to a consumable).

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.
